title: OmpF porin trimer in POPE/POPG bilayer — pore + L3 dynamics

C1.note: 2OMF fetched, trimer rebuilt via BIOMT (chains A/B/C), detergent C8E + crystal waters removed (raw_pdbs/2OMF_monomer_clean.pdb). POPE:POPG 3:1 bilayer via packmol-memgen. tLEaP build.in (ff19SB+lipid21+tip3p): leap.log reports Errors=0, total atoms 127082, Total unperturbed charge 0.000000 after addIons Cl- neutralized the +6 from ASH/GLH. (Minor: STUDY_REPORT cites 134,408 atoms vs leap's 127,082 — cosmetic inconsistency, build itself valid.)

C2.note: PLAN.md gives full tier-justified FF table: ff19SB (PMID 35294337, manual p.33), LIPID21 (PMID 35113553, p.49-51), TIP3P (LIPID21-matched, p.52), Joung-Cheatham K+/Cl- 0.15 M. Membrane flags justified: barostat=1 Berendsen (manual p.50 MC deforms bilayer), ntp=1 isotropic tensionless, cut=10. Staged restraint-release protocol, propka3 protonation rationale, lit review (Step 2b/2c), approval gate present. No hardcoded defaults.

C3.note: All stages present (min1/min2/heat/equil1-3/prod). prod.mdout AVERAGES: NSTEP=25000000 = 50 ns (dt 0.002), TEMP 310.03 K, Density 1.0545 g/cc, no NaN/blowup. Convergence: barrel RMSD drift 0.24 Å (<0.5), box area drift 0.06%. logs/prod exit 0.

C4.note: analysis.in: barrel-backbone alignment then L3 RMSD/RMSF, eyelet cross-channel distances on the correct constriction residues (acidic D113/E117 vs basic ladder R42/R82/R132), L3-zone Rg + tip radial displacement as pore proxies. Directly answers prompt (pore dims + L3 dynamics). Verified barrel RMSD mean 1.111 Å over 1000 frames. Minor: no HOLE/CHAP absolute radius profile (geometric proxies only, acknowledged in caveats §8).

C5.note: Paper (Cowan et al. Nature 1992, PMID 1380671, read) confirms: trimer of 16-stranded antiparallel beta-barrels, long internal loop (L3) constricting channel, charge distribution governing ion selectivity. Key expects trimer stable, pore ~7 Å, L3 constricts, barrel maintained. Agent reproduces all: stable trimer (RMSD 1.11 Å), barrel maintained, L3 rigid constriction (RMSF 0.50 Å < barrel 0.74), eyelet acidic-cluster-to-basic-ladder ~8.7-13 Å (CG/CD-CZ atom centers) consistent with the ~7x11 Å eyelet. Slight deduction: ~7 Å pore not measured as an explicit radius, only via residue-distance/Rg proxies.
